stories.title: Wednesday briefing: ​Do MPs have the will to stop mega-donors interfering in UK politics?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: The representation of the people bill has become a test of whether ​Britain can curb the surge in billionaire‑backed ​donations trying to influence how we run the country Good morning. I’m Hettie O’Brien, and I’ll be arriving in your inbox regularly from today, rounding out the First Edition team alongside Libby and Michael. I’ve spent much of my time at the Guardian writing about who is pulling the strings in Britain: I enjoy illuminating knotty subjects for our readers,

stories.title: Meloni’s hunting reform spells pressure at home and a clash with Brussels
stories.summary: ROME — Political competition is heating up on Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni’s right flank ahead of next year’s election — and her government’s proposed reform of hunting laws has become the latest battleground in Italy’s culture wars. The legislation, which would mark Italy’s biggest overhaul of hunting laws in more than three decades, would expand the rights of hunters by extending hunting grounds and seasons and increasing the number of huntable species. Most controversially, it would redefine

stories.title: Putin’s next move will be Baltic incursion, warns top Russian dissident
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin has no intention of striking a peace deal with Ukraine and is more likely to instead escalate his war after Moscow’s parliamentary election in September, according to Russian opposition activist Garry Kasparov. The dissident’s warning comes as Ukraine has stepped up attacks on Russian military logistics and energy infrastructure, a push that Estonian Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna said has made Russia “weaker” — and Kyiv hopes could give it the upper

stories.title: Watch: The clash between US and Iran for control of the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The BBC's Barbara Plett Usher examines the latest tensions over the key international shipping channel.

stories.title: Nemcsak az MCC, hanem más kekvák is megszűnnek július 31-én
stories.summary: A keddi Magyar Közlönyben jelent meg a lista.

stories.title: A nap, amikor utoljára szólt a magyarokról a foci-vb
stories.summary: Farkas János élete gólját rúgta a 60 évvel ezelőtti vb-n, Albert Flóriánt és Bene Ferencet nem tudták tartani, Mészöly Kálmán felkötött vállal is tanárian játszott. Elterjedt a legenda, hogy még a Beatles tagjai is személyesen gratuláltak a brazilokat fantasztikus játékkal 3:1-re legyőző magyar csapatnak Liverpoolban.

stories.title: A fideszes Szűcs Gábor egyszerre öt állással is elbírt, de életrajzot írni még nem volt ideje
stories.summary: A volt megafonos–alapjogokért központos–századvéges elemző-véleményvezér szerint a minisztériumi munkája mellett figyelt az összeférhetetlenségre, és csak tudományos tevékenységet végzett. Például a Pesti Srácok főszerkesztőjével beszélgetett Magyar Péterről.

stories.title: „Szemem, mint a sólyomé, fülem, mint a farkasé” – egy parlamenti anyázás hiteles története
stories.summary: Kulcsár Krisztián, a Tisza képviselője a parlament múlt heti ülésén szólt be durván Lezsák Annának az Alaptörvény 17. módosításának vitáján. A parlamenti jegyzőkönyv szerint a mi hazánkos Apáti István figyelmeztette, hogy tanúja volt mindennek, és próbálta bocsánatkérésre bírni. A bocsánatkérésből végül önkényezés lett, és előkerült Varga Judit neve is.

stories.title: Orbán János Dénes és az Operettszínház ügyeinek kivizsgálását kérik Tarr Zoltántól a színházi kritikusok
stories.summary: A Színházi Kritikusok Céhe nyílt levélben sürget vizsgálatot.

stories.title: Budapesten fogták el a kiemelten veszélyes német lövöldözőt
stories.summary: A német nyomozók több mint egy hónapja keresték nagy erőkkel a 33 éves férfit.

stories.title: Lech Wałęsa says it's a big mistake that Péter Magyar hasn't put any politicians in prison yet
stories.summary: The previous regime caused a great deal of harm to many Hungarians, according to the hero of Poland’s transition to democracy, which is why it must be made clear that they are held accountable. Telex spoke with Lech Wałęsa in Budapest.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor a családtagjai társaságában izgulta végig a francia–spanyol elődöntőt a dallasi stadionban
stories.summary: Az IShowSpeed nevű influenszer videós közvetítésében végig látszik, ahogy meccset néz a Fidesz elnöke. Még Orbán Sára férje is elkísérte.

stories.title: „Jaber már nem haver”, viccelődik a választás előtt a Fidesz mögé beálló kuvaiti–magyar repper
stories.summary: A repper néhány hónappal az országgyűlési választás előtt tűnt fel a Fidesz eseményein.

stories.title: F. Kovács Sándor: Gulyás Gergely érzi a felelősséget, hogy egy ilyen embert szabadított a magyar közéletre
stories.summary: A fideszes képviselő látta Gulyás Gergely vívódását, ami a frakcióvezető lemondásához vezetett. Még egy anekdotát is elmesélt Gulyásról, Magyar Péterről és a „szűk öltönyös csapatról”.

stories.title: Garaczi László: Az irodalom mentett meg egy tízéves depressziós időszakból
stories.summary: Hogyan lesz valaki katonatiszt szülők gyerekéből író? Milyen volt és mit adott a hetvenes-nyolcvanas évek budapesti undergroundja? Mit jelent ma annak a nemzedéknek a tagjaként élni, amely alapvetően megváltoztatta a magyar próza nyelvét? A hetvenéves Garaczi Lászlóval beszélgettünk.

stories.title: Lech Wałęsa szerint nagy hiba, hogy Magyar Péter még egy politikust sem zárt börtönbe
stories.summary: A lengyel rendszerváltás hőse szerint az előző rendszer rengeteg kárt okozott sok magyarnak, ezért meg kell mutatni, hogy megkapják a büntetésüket. Sulyok leváltása pedig szükséges ahhoz, hogy a demokrácia működni tudjon.

stories.title: Benyújtotta felmondását, távozik Orbán Ráhel barátnője az állami divatügynökség éléről
stories.summary: Jakab Zsófia azt írta, nem tudott megfelelő és konstruktív kommunikációt kialakítani az új tulajdonosi joggyakorlóval, vagyis Kapitány István minisztériumával.

stories.title: Zöld utat kapott az 1,8 milliárd forintos magyar állami támogatással épülő szálloda Kolozsváron
stories.summary: Jóváhagyta a helyi tanács a városrendezési tervet, amelynek alapján építési engedélyt kaphat a Kerékcsárda bővítése. Ez a beruházás kapná az egyik legnagyobb összeget a Pro Economica Alapítványon keresztül.

stories.title: Két és fél évnyi cigaretta van egy ilyen illegális eszközben, valóságos meghívó a mértéktelenségre
stories.summary: Illegális, addiktív és súlyos egészségügyi kockázatokat hordozhat az új vape, amely villámgyorsan terjed Magyarországon. Egy addiktológus szerint a több mint százezer slukkos eszköz növelheti a túlzott használat kockázatát.

stories.title: Nem tudta megverni az izlandi Víkingurt a Győr, nem lesz magyar csapat a Bajnokok Ligájában
stories.summary: A Bajnokok Ligája első selejtezőkörében kiesett a Győr, miután az izlandi Víkingur ellen egy hete az utolsó pillanatokban kapott egy gólt, itthon pedig nem tudott nyerni. A 2-2-es döntetlenről azt mondhatjuk: reális. A Konferencia-ligában folytathatja az ETO, feröeri vagy luxemburgi ellenfél vár rá.

stories.title: Semmi értelme rettegni attól, hogy mások cikinek tartanak
stories.summary: A brit gitárzenei színtér egyik legnagyobb csodabogara, a Black Midi egykori frontembere, Geordie Greep interjút adott a Telexnek. Szó esett Bonnie Blue-ról, a toxikus manoszféráról, zenésztársa haláláról, és arról is, hogy milyen a csimpánzok talpa.

stories.title: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office
stories.summary: Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years.

stories.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
stories.summary: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

stories.title: Iran launches strikes on neighbours as Trump threatens to hit power plants
stories.summary: Tehran's attack comes as the US military carried out a fourth night of strikes against Iran after President Donald Trump reinstated its blockade of Iranian shipping around the strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: No end in sight for new Iran war
stories.summary: As the U.S. war with Iran resumes, there is little sign that diplomacy can stop it. Efforts by Arab, Pakistani and other mediators to revive negotiations or restore a ceasefire have shown no public signs of progress, and the overall feeling in the Middle East and beyond is that the fighting will simply continue for now, according to two analysts and a person familiar with the situation. The White House is “not really sure where this is headed,” said a former U.S. official in touch with Trum

stories.title: Singapore court orders Bloomberg to pay ministers $356,000 in defamation case
stories.summary: Bloomberg’s editor-in-chief, John Micklethwait, stands by reporting, saying ministers who sued ‘imposed an extremely strained meaning on what was a solid ​story’ Bloomberg News and one of its reporters have been ordered to pay S$460,000 (US$355,734) in damages after an article it published was found to have defamed two Singapore government ministers, the city-state’s high court said in a judgment released on Tuesday. Bloomberg and the reporter, Low De Wei, are liable to jointly pay S$230,000 to

stories.title: US Fifth Fleet targeted in Bahrain, says Iran, in latest exchange of strikes – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is closed. You can read our latest full report here Resurgent oil and fuel prices could cement a fourth interest rate rise in Australia this year if Donald Trump’s renewed conflict with Iran is not resolved within a week , economists warn. US missile strikes on Iran and Trump’s announcement of a new maritime blockade has lifted oil prices to their highest point in the month since the two countries agreed to a peace deal . Continue reading...

stories.title: Alleged illegal donations by Catholic Schools NSW to Liberals raise questions over use of taxpayer funds
stories.summary: Catholic Schools NSW’s alleged donations to Liberal party show the need to ensure public money is spent for approved purposes, expert says Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An education funding expert says alleged illegal donations by Catholic Schools NSW to Liberal party operatives, under investigation by the state corruption watchdog, show governments need to check taxpayer funding was “being spent according to i

stories.title: Trump threatens to expand strikes on Iran’s civilian infrastructure
stories.summary: US president says he will knock out all power plants and bridges in effort to force Tehran to agree to deal amid impasse over strait Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has threatened to expand US strikes on Iran next week to target power plants and bridges if Tehran does not agree to a deal amid a continuing dispute over the strait of Hormuz. “Next week it gets really bad for them because next week comes the power plants. Next week comes the bridges,” the US president said in a Fox N

stories.title: Australia news live: Shoebridge criticises Labor for delay in responding to AI; five-year-old dead after crash in Perth
stories.summary: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Shorten shocked by testimony given to royal commission on antisemitism Former Labor leader Bill Shorten , now the vice-chancellor of the University of Canberra, said he was shocked by the evidence given from the university sector during the latest round of hearings before the royal commission on antisemitism. It describes an Australia … and Australian universities that I don’t recognise. But the fact is that th

stories.title: Ursula von der Leyen in Kyiv to announce new defence initiatives as Ukraine and Russia exchange strikes – Europe live
stories.summary: In Brussels EU ambassadors will be trying to work out the details of the 21st sanctions package for Russia European Commission president Ursula von der Leyen is in Kyiv today with plans to announce “new initiatives to integrate our defence industries,” a day after both European and Ukrainian armies were involved in the Bastille Day parade in Paris. “It’s a special moment. Ukraine has built a strong military momentum. The tide is turning, ” she said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump threatens strikes on Iranian bridges, power plants
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has warned that US strikes will escalate and target key infrastructure if Tehran does not make a deal. The US has reimposed a naval blockade and Iran has said the Strait of Hormuz is closed.

stories.title: Bill to make daylight saving time permanent passes US House vote: ‘Americans are tired of the clock change’
stories.summary: Sunshine Protection Act has bipartisan support, including backing of Trump and some Democratic co-sponsors, and will head to the Senate next The US House of Representatives on Tuesday voted overwhelmingly to approve a bill that would end the practice of changing clocks twice a year and make daylight saving time permanent. The bid to end clock-changing, dubbed the Sunshine Protection Act, has bipartisan support, including the backing of Donald Trump and some Democratic co-sponsors. Following the

stories.title: US marshal shot dead serving arrest warrant in Louisiana
stories.summary: Clarence Frazier Jr charged with murder over killing of Drew Hanson and could face death penalty if convicted A federal agent was fatally shot while trying to help authorities capture a man who failed to show up to his sexual battery trial in Louisiana , according to the US justice department. Clarence Frazier Jr, 48, was charged with murder in the killing of deputy US marshal Drew Hanson, authorities said. Frazier could face life imprisonment or the death penalty if he is eventually convicted o

stories.title: Melbourne council votes to scrap Lime ebikes for failing to meet ‘bare minimum standards’
stories.summary: City of Yarra councillors end trial, claiming the company has not done enough to stop dumping and misuse of share bikes Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Shared ebikes will disappear from some inner-city Melbourne streets after a council scrapped its agreement with Australia’s largest electric bike operator. The City of Yarra on Tuesday voted to end its memorandum of understanding with Lime, terminating its almost

stories.title: Labour must stop just writing a cheque for benefit claimants, says McFadden
stories.summary: Exclusive: Work and pensions secretary signals possible reform to welfare as ministers await key reviews Labour must stop “simply writing a cheque” for health and disability benefit claimants and will provide more job support instead, the work and pensions secretary has said. Pat McFadden said the government was preparing to launch a renewed effort at welfare reform with a focus on encouraging more people with health conditions to get into work and off benefits. Continue reading...

stories.title: University of Melbourne ‘sharply’ changed protest policies after pro-Palestine sit-ins, commission hears
stories.summary: Royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion hears of response of universities to incidents on campus Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The interim vice-chancellor of the University of Melbourne (UoM) says pro-Palestine sit-ins on campus led to the institution “quite sharply” changing its protest policies and that further restrictions could arise. Prof Glyn Davis appeared before the royal commission into

stories.title: Brisbane child accused of school stabbing allegedly faced ‘racist’ abuse, court hears
stories.summary: Alleged attack may have been motivated by a social media post, police prosecutor tells court Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Brisbane child accused of stabbing a fellow student was allegedly subjected to months of “racist” abuse and threats, a court has heard. The 16-year-old child was arrested after allegedly stabbing a 17-year-old schoolmate on Tuesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Spanish PM’s brother banned from public office after misconduct trial
stories.summary: Conviction over hiring by socialist-led council is one of a series of corruption claims facing Pedro Sánchez’s family The brother of Spain’s prime minister has been banned from holding public office for nine years after being found guilty of administrative misconduct relating to his hiring by a socialist-led council in the south-western region of Extremadura nine years ago. Corruption allegations involving Pedro Sánchez’s family, his government and his Spanish Socialist Workers’ party (PSOE) hav

stories.title: EU rejects Trump administration claims that ICC threatens US sovereignty
stories.summary: US government says it wants to ‘systematically disable’ The Hague-based international criminal court A spokesperson for the EU has pushed back against the Trump administration’s assertion that the international criminal court poses a threat to US sovereignty, a day after the US government said it would work to “systematically disable” a global tribunal that seeks to prosecute the perpetrators of the world’s gravest crimes. Anouar El Anouni, an EU spokesperson, said on Tuesday: “We stand firm in

stories.title: Trump withdraws Hormuz tolls threat but says US will continue to blockade Iran
stories.summary: US hits targets in port cities of Bushehr and Bandar Abbas while Iran targets Bahrain and Jordan in retaliation Middle East crisis live – latest updates Donald Trump has backed down from a threat that ships would have to pay a 20% fee to the US for “security” in the strait of Hormuz, replacing it with what he described as investment and trade deals with Gulf Arab states as US and Iranian airstrikes resumed for a third day. The US president said he had decided to scrap the toll “based on highly p

stories.title: India's 'most ambitious' trade deal with UK takes effect
stories.summary: A landmark free trade agreement allows zero-duty market access for nearly 99% of India's exports to the UK. Meanwhile, US senators have proposed a new bill to impose 100% tariffs on India over Russian oil purchases.

stories.title: Bastille Day: France holds parade with Ukraine in focus
stories.summary: The celebrations featured troops and warplanes from around Europe in a show of support for Ukraine, as it fends off Russia's ongoing invasion. It was Emmanuel Macron's last Bastille Day as France's president.

stories.title: How a former 'street kid' is key to South Africa's police corruption inquiry
stories.summary: Vusimusi "Cat" Matlala, due to appear at the Madlanga Commission, grew up hustling to make a living.

stories.title: Cuba plunged into third nationwide blackout in two weeks as power grid collapses
stories.summary: Fuel has been scarce across Cuba since January, when US President Donald Trump threatened to impose tariffs on any nation that supplies or sells oil to the island, exacerbating the island's continuing financial and economic crises.

stories.title: 'We will deal with it,' Trump says as US probes reports of Iranian drones in Cuba
stories.summary: The US president said his administration is checking whether Tehran stores drones and missiles on the island and warned Washington will act if confirmed, but he offered no proof or details about the alleged weapons.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthis launch missiles at Saudi Arabia after strikes on Sanaa airport
stories.summary: The Iran-backed Houthis blame Saudi Arabia for the attack in Yemen's capital and say they targeted Abha airport in response.

stories.title: US strikes Bushehr as Iran's parliament tables bill to control Hormuz
stories.summary: Four points in the port city hosting Iran's only nuclear power plant were hit by US projectiles on Tuesday, as Iran's parliament formally submitted a bill asserting Tehran's permanent control over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
stories.summary: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".

stories.title: New polling shows Brits aren’t buying Nigel Farage’s anti-establishment crusade
stories.summary: LONDON — Brits are skeptical of Nigel Farage’s claim that the political establishment is out to get him and reckon his by-election gambit will backfire, according to new polling conducted for POLITICO. A majority said the Reform UK leader’s decision to resign his Clacton seat to give locals a referendum over mounting questions about his finances is a pointless waste of time and money, the new survey by Public First shows. Respondents across the U.K. also rated the satirical candidate known as Co

stories.title: Supreme court justices request $14.6m increase in security amid rise in threats
stories.summary: Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett appear before House lawmakers in rare testimony to ask for more protection Supreme court justice Amy Coney Barrett told House lawmakers that a sharp rise in threats against her and other justices is increasingly affecting her personal and family lives. Barrett and fellow supreme court justice Elena Kagan made the case for increased security in rare House testimony to discuss the court’s budget request. The last time a sitting justice answered questions on Capito

stories.title: Seven Britons among 12 foreign nationals killed in Spain’s deadly wildfires
stories.summary: Fire broke out last Thursday in the southeastern province of Almería, which is home to many foreign residents Seven Britons are among 12 foreign nationals killed in wildfires in southern Spain, authorities said. Officials said 12 of the 13 victims were foreign nationals after completing postmortem examinations after the fires that swept through Andalusia. Continue reading...

stories.title: Florida executes one of its oldest prisoners in state’s 10th lethal injection this year
stories.summary: Man who was 74 was one of three older prisoners scheduled for execution within the span of a month Florida put to death one of its oldest prisoners in its state history on Tuesday, a 74-year-old convicted murderer who was one of three older prisoners scheduled for execution within the span of a month in the nation’s busiest death penalty state. Dennis Sochor was pronounced dead at 6.16pm after a three-drug injection at Florida state prison near Starke. He was convicted of killing Patricia Giffor

stories.title: One person dead and two missing after boat sinks near San Francisco’s Alcatraz Island
stories.summary: Another 16 people rescued after ‘pontoon pleasure boat’ sinks about 600 yards from famous tourist attraction One person was killed, two were missing and 16 were rescued after a pontoon boat capsized and sank near San Francisco’s Alcatraz Island on Tuesday, authorities said. Nineteen people were aboard the vessel at the time of the incident. Continue reading...

stories.title: Japan officials hunt bear that raided couple’s fridge amid string of break-ins
stories.summary: Officials set up traps and electric fences after 14 break-ins recored in one town in two weeks, amid fears of a repeat offender Authorities are searching for a bear that sneaked into the home of an elderly couple and raided their fridge amid concerns it may be behind 14 break-ins across a Japanese town in the past fortnight. On Monday evening, Mitsuo Matsubara, 87, was confronted by a large Asiatic black bear when he went to investigate a noise in his kitchen. His fridge was open , and food was

stories.title: England risks building new ‘death traps’ as experts warn of overheating crisis
stories.summary: Vulnerable people more at risk as research finds only half of local authority plans require cooling strategies England risks constructing a new generation of “death trap” buildings that can fatally overheat unless the government tightens standards and prioritises climate safeguards, planning experts have said. Fears are growing about the plight of vulnerable people in heatwaves, with research this week suggesting that 2,700 people had died in the May and June heatwaves in England and Wales. Yet

stories.title: Andy Burnham urged to overhaul ‘timid and limited’ elections bill
stories.summary: Ex-minister who helped write draft calls for bolder action on voting reform, crypto donations and social media The government was “timid” and “incremental” when deciding what to include in its elections bill, a former minister who helped write it has said, as she urged the incoming prime minister, Andy Burnham, to go further. Rushanara Ali, who resigned as democracy minister last August, said the draft legislation still contained big gaps when it came to voting reform, cryptocurrency donations a

stories.title: Starmer faces final Cabinet and PMQs as Burnham nears Labour leadership finish line – UK politics live
stories.summary: The outgoing Labour leader will have final talks with his senior ministers in No 10 before taking his last PMQs Good morning. Everyone likes a leaving do and today it’s Keir Starmer’s, or at least his parliamentary one. If the colleague who is going is someone you like and admire, then the chance to see them thanked and celebrated is welcome. And if the colleague who is going is someone that you don’t like, or who has stayed too long, then that’s even better. At a good leaving do, there are also

stories.title: ‘God is punishing the politicians’: anger at earthquake response grows in Venezuela
stories.summary: Threat of social unrest rises as public indignation at lack of disaster aid comes on top of fallout from US military intervention • A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit Venezuela Public anger at what many perceive as the Venezuelan government’s botched response to twin earthquakes that killed nearly 4,500 people is growing, with one grieving mother caught on camera berating the son of former president Nicolás Maduro. Maduro’s politician son received a hos

stories.title: Iranian flights to Yemen are violation of sovereignty, says Yemeni official
stories.summary: Plane bringing Houthi delegation home from Tehran had to divert after Yemeni government bombed Sana’a airport Middle East crisis live – latest updates Iranian flights to and from Yemen are an unacceptable violation of the country’s sovereignty, the vice-president in Yemen’s Saudi-backed, UN-recognised government has said. Abdullah al-Alimi said in an interview that the planes contained equipment for the Houthi movement, which he said had transformed from merely a domestic threat into a regional

stories.title: France holds annual Bastille Day parade in Paris – video
stories.summary: France marked its annual Bastille Day celebration in Paris with a parade of military regiments, including foreign troops. Leaders from the 'coalition of the willing' were invited to watch the parade Continue reading...

stories.title: Young Germans opting out of military service as Berlin strives to boost army
stories.summary: Almost 6,000 young men apply to be excluded on moral or religious grounds despite ‘conscription lite’ policy The number of young men applying to be conscientious objectors and refuse armed military service in Germany has risen sharply this year, undermining a drive by Berlin to create Europe’s strongest conventional army and deter the Russian threat. More people had applied to exclude themselves from service on religious or moral grounds in the first half of 2026 than in the whole of last year,

stories.title: Keir Starmer becomes first UK PM to receive France’s Légion d’honneur
stories.summary: Exclusive: Emmanuel Macron honours outgoing prime minister for leadership role in supporting Ukraine Keir Starmer has become the first UK prime minister to be presented with the Légion d’honneur by a French president, in recognition of his work with France on the security of Europe. Emmanuel Macron awarded the historic honour to Starmer for his leadership in setting up the coalition of the willing – a group of countries chaired by France and the UK that have pledged to support Ukraine – at a cri

stories.title: Bastille Day celebrations in France tempered by fear of more wildfires
stories.summary: Firework displays cancelled as Paris military parade asserts ‘France’s rearmament … and Europe’s strategic awakening’ Emmanuel Macron has presided over his final Bastille Day parade in Paris amid a searing heatwave and wildfires that forced authorities around the country to cancel traditional firework displays and balls celebrating France’s national day. The French president was joined for the annual military procession and flypast by his Ukrainian counterpart, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, the British ‌

stories.title: Dua Lipa calls Albanian protests against Jared Kushner-backed resort ‘inspiring’
stories.summary: Pop star, whose parents are Kosovan Albanian, expresses admiration for demonstrations, which are entering their sixth week Albania’s “flamingo revolution” has won its most prominent supporter yet after the pop star Dua Lipa expressed admiration for the protest movement against a Trump family-backed resort in the Balkan state. As demonstrations against the €1.6bn (£1.36bn) real estate project entered a sixth week, the London-born singer, who was partially raised in Pristina, home to her Kosovan A

stories.title: Border controls scrapped between Spain and Gibraltar
stories.summary: As the end of border controls took effect, the old chain-link fence between Spain and Gibraltar was taken down. This historic decision allows for greater freedom of movement for people and goods.

stories.title: 150 years of the Bayreuth Festival: Confronting a troubled legacy
stories.summary: Should the past be confronted — or avoided? Why the Wagner festival's handling of its Nazi-era legacy remains a contentious issue in its 150th anniversary year.

stories.title: Volodymyr Zelenskyy pushes for new government in Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko stepped down after barely a year in office. President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is already in talks with potential successors. What can Ukraine expect from the Cabinet reshuffle?

stories.title: Iran war: US launches new round of strikes
stories.summary: The US military said the strikes were "to continue degrading Iranian capabilities." Iran meanwhile has said the US renewed blockade "dismantles" the truce deal.

stories.title: How the 2016 coup attempt changed Turkey for good
stories.summary: Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military attempted to overthrow the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. The consequences of the failed coup continue to shape Turkey to this day. DW looks back.

stories.title: French and Spanish fans pack Texas streets ahead World Cup semi-final
stories.summary: French and Spanish fans flooded Arlington’s streets ahead the World Cup semi-final, with the winners to meet England or Argentina.

stories.title: Wife describes moment husband nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane
stories.summary: A man was nearly sucked head-first out of a cabin window mid-air on a Ryanair plane.

stories.title: Pygmy hippo in Thailand predicts France vs Argentina World Cup final
stories.summary: Pygmy hippo Moo Deng at a Thai zoo predicts a France–Argentina World Cup final, choosing France over Spain and Argentina over England in a playful stunt.

stories.title: From Wimbledon towels to Scotch: What India-UK trade deal could mean for shoppers
stories.summary: The free trade agreement between the world's fifth and sixth largest economies has come into effect - can it deliver?

stories.title: US Congress takes next step to make daylight saving time permanent
stories.summary: The House of Representatives passed a Trump-backed bill that would end decades of Americans having to reset their clocks twice a year.

stories.title: Trump retreat over Hormuz tolls suggests he is struggling to end Iran war
stories.summary: The abrupt about-face from the US president was the latest twist in a conflict that has now lasted more than four months.

stories.title: Seven Britons among those killed in Spain wildfires
stories.summary: Twelve of the 13 victims were foreign nationals, Spanish authorities said on Tuesday.

stories.title: China detains US scientist who studied North Korea nuclear tests
stories.summary: Chen Youlin's family says he has been wrongfully detained for nearly two years on spying charges.

stories.title: China economic growth falls sharply, missing target
stories.summary: Weak demand domestically and the impact of the Iran war on oil prices overshadowed the country's strong exports.

stories.title: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
stories.summary: Trump's comments aired as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth day in a row and the US resumed blockading Iranian ports.

stories.title: Sudan's war economy fuelling 'self-perpetuating' conflict, UN human rights office says

stories.title: Singapore court orders Bloomberg to pay $356,000 to ministers in defamation case
stories.summary: The ministers claimed the piece, which cited their luxury bungalow deals, had tarnished their reputation.

stories.title: Trump pays writer E Jean Carroll $5m in damages over sexual abuse and defamation
stories.summary: The president had sought to delay the payment as he tried to persuade the Supreme Court to overturn the judgement.

stories.title: Japan admits growing need to counter espionage after Russian ‘den of spies’ report
stories.summary: Issue must be addressed with ‘even greater rigour’, says government spokesman, after New York Times report on how it has become a spy hub for Vladimir Putin Japan has said it recognised the need to counter foreign intelligence better after the New York Times reported that Russia had turned the country into a “den of spies” and key source of weapons components. The newspaper, in an investigation published on Sunday, reported that thanks to “weak espionage laws”, Moscow was using Japan as a key hu

stories.title: Sweden prides itself on equality – so why is its political gender gap growing?
stories.summary: As general election looms, survey shows twice as many men as women support far-right Sweden Democrats One is led by Sweden’s first female prime minister, Magdalena Andersson , and has promised smaller school-class sizes, more housing and free dental care for young people. The other, led by Jimmie Åkesson , has neo-Nazi roots and has pledged to lower taxes, improve public safety and treat “anti-Swedishness” as a hate crime. In the run-up to Sweden’s general election in September, the Social Democ

stories.title: China’s monthly car ‌exports top 1m for first time as overall trade soars
stories.summary: Country risks new tariffs from US and EU as it looks likely to match or beat last year’s record surplus of $1tn Business live – latest updates China’s monthly car ‌exports topped 1m for the first time in June as overall overseas shipments from the world’s second biggest economy rose 27%. Official Chinese customs data showed that a stronger-than-expected trade performance kept China on track to match or beat last year’s record trade surplus of $1tn (£748bn), achieved despite Donald Trump’s curtai

stories.title: New US Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment
stories.summary: Aid worker flown to Berlin as Trump administration bars Americans from traveling to US on commercial flights A US national who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has arrived in Germany for treatment, the health ministry in Berlin said on Monday, weeks after another American infected with Ebola in the DRC was treated in Berlin. Meanwhile, the Trump administration on Monday said it was blocking American citizens in ⁠the DRC from traveling to the US on commercial flights, Reut

stories.title: Bangkok bar fire: death toll reaches 30 as police say negligence is ‘primary theory’
stories.summary: Bar owner offers ‘deepest apologies’ as police investigate whether exits were either blocked or hard to access The Bangkok pub that became the scene of the city’s deadliest blaze in 17 years has said it will cooperate with an investigation into alleged negligence, as the death toll rose to 30. The local district office said on Tuesday that three more people had died after the devastating fire that broke out in the early hours of Monday. An initial assessment by disaster officials found an electr

stories.title: France deploys 70,000 security personnel across country for Bastille Day, World Cup semifinal events – as it happened
stories.summary: ‘No unruly behaviour will be tolerated’ after the match, according to France’s interior minister Here is the first big moment of the day as the Garde républicaine plays the French national anthem, “La Marseillaise”. Macron is not singing, but Lecornu behind him – very much is. Continue reading...

stories.title: Derision at US chain Chipotle’s plan to sell its Mexican food in Mexico
stories.summary: One social media user described the opening of its first outlet in Monterrey as ‘like the dog teaching a duck to fly’ A US chain’s plan to sell its version of Mexican food to Mexicans with a first branch south of the border has prompted bemusement, skepticism and anger among local people. Chipotle Mexican Grill, known for its customisable burritos, tacos and bowls, has more than 4,000 locations worldwide and announced on Tuesday that it was expanding into Mexico in what it described as a signifi

stories.title: France arrests arson suspects amid Fontainebleau forest fire
stories.summary: Firefighters are attempting to tackle a major fire burning since Sunday in the much-visited Fontainebleau forest, near France's capital Paris. Two people have been arrested for suspected arson.

stories.title: Germany news: Military conscientious objections spike
stories.summary: The number of people applying for conscientious objection to military service has risen sharply and there were around 5,100 heat-related deaths recorded in June alone.

stories.title: Ukraine targets another 11 Russian ships in Sea of Azov
stories.summary: Ukraine said it struck tankers and cargo vessels in the Sea of Azov, in the latest strikes on Russian commercial shipping. Moscow's Agriculture Ministry said "alternative shipping routes" are being sought.

stories.title: Micronutrients are good, a balanced diet is better
stories.summary: Chronic illnesses, heart disease, obesity ― we know eating too much of the wrong foods can be unhealthy. But what about "good" foods: Is modern farming diluting micronutrients in our fruit and vegetables?

stories.title: Nigeria offers clues to new US Africa strategy
stories.summary: The withdrawal of US troops from Nigeria after the killing of an "Islamic State" leader may be less about leaving Africa than redefining how Washington fights terrorism on the continent.

stories.title: As the world rearms, South Korea aims to cash in
stories.summary: Weapons and military equipment produced in South Korea are sought-after due to their reliability, speedy delivery times and relatively low prices. Global demand for weapons has surged after Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

stories.title: Ukraine parliament accepts PM Svyrydenko's resignation
stories.summary: With Ukraine "changing its political strategy," President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is trying to remake the government,

stories.title: Ebola outbreak much larger than official figures, WHO says
stories.summary: The outbreak, which has infected almost 2,000 people and killed more than 700, could be at least two to four times larger than official figures, the UN health agency warned. The current outbreak is in the DRC and Uganda.

stories.title: US-Iraq relations: Can Iran-allied militias be disarmed?
stories.summary: When Donald Trump meets Iraq's new PM today, they are likely to discuss disarming Iran's allies in Iraq. Some have already pledged to give up their guns. But is that likely? And will it influence the US-Iran war?

stories.title: T. rex 'Gus' sells for record-breaking $50.1 million
stories.summary: The Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton surpassed its pre-sale estimate and set a new auction record for a dinosaur fossil.

stories.title: Iranian newspaper threatens German Chancellor Merz
stories.summary: A major Iranian newspaper has published a threat of "retaliation" against German Chancellor Friedrich Merz. Politicians have expressed concern, and the German government has issued a cautious response.

stories.title: US troop withdrawal from Iraq set for September 30
stories.summary: The planned US withdrawal is linked to the disarmament of influential Iran-backed militias in Iraq.

stories.title: How India's Lord's win echoes the legacy of its pioneering women cricketers
stories.summary: India won the first women's Test at Lord's, 142 years after its first men's Test.

stories.title: EU bans gold imports from Sudan to curb money financing the war
stories.summary: The ban also restricts exports of mercury and cyanide to Sudan, chemicals widely used in gold mining.

stories.title: Spectacular drones and fireworks illuminate Eiffel Tower ahead of Bastille Day
stories.summary: Paris staged an early Eiffel Tower fireworks and drone show, reserving Bastille Day to commemorate the 2016 Nice attack and its 86 victims.

stories.title: Zelenskyy joins Macron and European leaders in Paris for Bastille Day
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy joins Emmanuel Macron and European leaders at a Ukraine-focused Bastille Day parade in Paris showcasing military strength.

stories.title: US-Iran conflict widens after deadly tanker attack in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Commercial shipping has come under attack in the Strait of Hormuz as the US and Iran exchange military action, raising fears the conflict could draw more Gulf states into the fighting.

stories.title: Diamond giant De Beers halts work at flagship South African mine as demand plummets
stories.summary: Production will stop for two years at the mine which employs more than 4,000 people.

stories.title: Tyrannosaurus rex most expensive dinosaur ever sold
stories.summary: The dinosaur skeleton, which roamed the planet 67 million years ago, was sold by Sotheby's in New York.

stories.title: World leaders gather in Doha to honour Qatar's Father Emir Sheikh Hamad
stories.summary: Leaders from Europe, Africa and Asia travelled to Doha to offer condolences to the royal family following the death of Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, who served as Qatar's emir from 1995 until 2013.

stories.title: Colombian national killed by ICE agent during operation in Maine

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz 'faultline' exposes weakness of the US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Control of the waterway has become a key point of contention between the US and Iran, as a ceasefire deal falters.

stories.title: 'If we die, we die together': Wife of man nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane speaks of ordeal
stories.summary: Svetlana Grković told Serbian media that her husband is "seriously injured and in shock".

stories.title: Egyetlen pletyka elég volt, összeomlott egy amerikai elektromosautó-gyártó árfolyama
stories.summary: Nagyot estek kedden a Lucid részvényei, miután egy szaklap arról számolt be, hogy a cég fontolgatja a tőzsdei kivezetés vagy a csődvédelem lehetőségét.

stories.title: Húsz év után nem adományoz többet Warren Buffett Bill Gates alapítványának
stories.summary: A milliárdos néhány héttel azután döntött így, hogy a Microsoft-alapító részletesen beszámolt a Jeffrey Epsteinnel való kapcsolatáról.

stories.title: Spanyolország nem adott esélyt a franciáknak, 16 év után játszhat vb-döntőt
stories.summary: Spanyolország 2-0-ra nyerte a világbajnokság első elődöntőjét, miután mindkét félidőben rúgott egy gólt, és teljesen eltüntette a félelmetes francia támadósort, ami sokáig a kaput sem találta el. Az Európa-bajnok spanyolok várják vasárnapi ellenfelüket: Angliát vagy Argentínát.

stories.title: Túl vagyunk a kakaósokkon, de van egy nyomós oka annak, hogy mégsem lesz olcsóbb a csoki: mi magunk
stories.summary: Ha valaki négy éve rendszeresen vett egy tábla mogyorós tejcsokit, ma könnyen meglepődhet a pénztárnál. Egy átlagos tábla ára nagyjából megduplázódott, ahogy a bolti ár többéves késéssel követte a történelmi csúcsra dráguló kakaó árfolyamát. De most hiába zuhan a kakaó ára, mégsem lesz olcsóbb a csoki a boltokban. A szomorú igazság ugyanis, hogy a kereslet rugalmatlan: azaz nincs értelme csökkenteni a csoki árát, mert bármennyibe kerül, úgyis meg akarjuk enni.

stories.title: A világ legnagyobb kamiongyártója hadiipari nyitással vészelné át az autóipar válságát

stories.summary: A franciák szövetségi kapitánya büszke arra, amit elértek, és azt már korábban lehetett tudni, hogy a világbajnokság után mindenképpen távozik a posztjáról.

stories.title: Mbappé: Nem úgy játszottunk, ahogy szerettünk volna
stories.summary: A franciák csapatkapitánya kíméletlenül önkritikus volt, szerinte a játékuk nem volt méltó egy vb-elődöntőhöz.

stories.title: Luis de la Fuente: Ezek a játékosok a nehéz feladatokat is könnyedén végzik el
stories.summary: Az első gólt szerző Oyarzabal azt mondta, tudták, hogy nyugodtan kell játszaniuk, mert így bármikor veszélyesek lehetnek.

stories.summary: Számottevő csapadék azért szerdán sem várható.

stories.summary: A Velencei-tó szerda reggel 41 centisre apadt Agárdnál, a Duna Esztergomnál negatívba fordult: –11 centit mértek.

In his first term he repeated this charge, threatening to withdraw from Nato and berating US allies around the world in the process. Last week’s gathering of Nato’s heads of government in Turkey suggests his approach is runni

stories.title: The Guardian view on Volkswagen’s crisis: another wake-up call for Germany and the EU | Editorial
stories.summary: Robust action is needed to protect European industries from unfair competition. The alternative is social strife amid growing insecurity According to a recent analysis , China enjoys a surplus in its manufactured goods trade with the European Union that is roughly equivalent to Italy’s national income. That trade disparity, it is estimated, continues to grow by about 30% each year. The stark implication, according to a paper from Centre for European Reform, is that Europe, with Germany in the fr

stories.title: Mexico to demand criminal complaints over migrants killed by ICE in US
stories.summary: Claudia Sheinbaum says Mexicans ‘outraged’ over killing last week of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo by agents in Houston Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Monday that Mexico would be demanding criminal complaints in the US for the deaths of more than a dozen Mexican migrants in immigration detention and those killed in anti-migrant operations. The deaths include last week’s killing of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston, whom Sheinbaum said was “practically murdered”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump says US reinstating blockade on Iranian ports – as it happened

stories.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
stories.summary: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

stories.title: Germany set to restrict its Freedom of Information Act
stories.summary: Critics fear the reforms could weaken government transparency by curbing civil society's access to official information and limiting requests from foreigners.

stories.title: Peace is possible, Aliyev tells global media forum in Shusha
stories.summary: Azerbaijan's president also told the Shusha Global Media Forum that long-term gas contracts were needed for Baku to expand production, and said that Baku was considering leaving the Council of Europe.

stories.title: Health fears as frail Indian activist on hunger strike loses 8.2kg

stories.title: Bangkok fire investigation finds locked doors and flammable decor as deaths climb to 30
stories.summary: Survivors and first responders reported locked doors and a lack of signage marking emergency exits.

stories.title: Trump watches IndyCar pit stop at the White House
stories.summary: President Donald Trump watched an IndyCar pit stop demonstration at the White House as he promoted the inaugural Freedom 250 Grand Prix, a race set to take place around Washington's National Mall next month.

stories.title: China's June exports surge 27% from a year earlier as AI boom drives strong demand
stories.summary: China’s exports accelerated in June, jumping 27% from a year earlier thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence, the customs agency said on Tuesday.

stories.title: US releases video of sea drone strike on Iranian naval base
stories.summary: The US military has released video it says shows three Corsair unmanned surface vessels striking a ship maintenance facility and submarine at Iran's Bandar Abbas Naval Base on 12 July. US Central Command says it was the first combat use of American sea drones.

stories.title: US launches more strikes on Iran as renewed standoff over Strait of Hormuz intensifies
stories.summary: Iranian state media reported US strikes targeting large areas across southern and western Iran, including Qeshm island and Bandar Abbas near the strait, and in Khuzestan province bordering Iraq. Missile alert sirens also sounded at dawn Monday in Bahrain, home to the US Navy's 5th Fleet.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Unusually warm rivers affect French nuclear power plants
stories.summary: High temperatures and below average rainfall put pressure on waterways used to cool reactors Above average temperatures combined with below average rainfall across much of western and central Europe during June and the first half of July have placed increasing pressure on rivers, ecosystems and energy infrastructure. Persistent high pressure brought prolonged sunshine, suppressed rainfall and enhanced evaporation, causing river levels to fall and water temperatures to increase. These unusually w

stories.title: Killings continue on Del Monte farm in Kenya, families say, after G4S hired for security
stories.summary: Exclusive: Three men killed in incidents over past year allegedly involving G4S guards, who replaced in-house team after previous deaths Bereaved families and politicians have raised alarm about continued killings on Del Monte’s pineapple farm in Kenya despite the company hiring a British security firm to replace its in-house security team after previous deaths were exposed by the Guardian. The multinational food company appointed G4S to guard the farm, which is estimated to cover at least 40 sq

stories.title: Kerítésszaggató, bójarágcsáló, forgalombénító fókába szeretett bele a fél világ
stories.summary: Fókamóka a köbön: Neil partra szállt Tasmaniában, és rombolni kezdte a környezetét, de ezzel inkább rajongókat szerzett. Az állatvédők féltik, nehogy konfliktusba keveredjen az emberekkel, és Freya, az agyonlőtt norvég rozmár sorsára jusson. Neil most elúszott az óceánba, de várhatóan még nagyobbként tér vissza szülőhelyére.

stories.summary: A módosítás a veszélyhelyzet megszűnésével függ össze, a miniszter mostantól nem jelölheti ki akkumulátor-újrafeldolgozó üzemek helyszínét.

stories.summary: A lakóknak sikerült kijutniuk az épületből, de három kutyájuk és több tucat madaruk is elpusztult a tűzben.

stories.summary: Albániában hetek óta tart a Jared Kushner gigaberuházása elleni „flamingós forradalom”.

stories.summary: A spanyol válogatott jutott a foci-vb döntőjébe, miután simán legyőzte az egész vb-n lehengerlően játszó franciákat. Franciaországnak komoly helyzete sem volt a mérkőzésen.

stories.summary: Ezt a Fidesz kommunikációs igazgatója is megerősítette a HVG-nek.

stories.title: EU experts urge social media restrictions for kids under 13
stories.summary: An expert panel has recommended limiting access to social media for young children across the EU. The European Commission is expected to present a proposal for legislation on the issue in the coming months.

stories.title: Germany's Ahr Valley: Rebuilding better after deadly flood
stories.summary: New walls, redesigned bridges and restored floodplains: Towns in a small German valley are testing how they can survive the next extreme flood.

stories.title: Azerbaijan considering full withdrawal from Council of Europe, Aliyev says
stories.summary: President Ilham Aliyev said Baku was considering full withdrawal from the 46-member organisation after Azerbaijan's voting rights in PACE were suspended in 2024.

stories.title: People flee Bangkok bar as huge flames shoot out from doorway
stories.summary: At least 28 people have died and 25 are critically injured after a fire tore through a bar in a popular Bangkok district.

stories.title: Drone footage shows devastation after deadly Spain wildfire
stories.summary: Drone footage shows the devastation left by a deadly wildfire in southern Spain that killed at least 12 people and burned around 7,000 hectares. Authorities say the blaze has now been brought under control.

stories.title: The SpaceX IPO made history. One month on has it lost momentum?
stories.summary: A month on from the firm's stock market debut, the reality of how SpaceX currently makes money has seemed to come into clearer focus.

stories.title: A versatile actor whose roles went far beyond Jurassic Park - Sam Neill dies aged 78
stories.summary: The actor built up a long string of screen credits over a five-decade career.

stories.title: Video shows civilians taking cover during Russian strike on Sumy
stories.summary: Four people were injured after a Russian strike hit Odesa on 13 July 2026, according to Ukraine's State Emergency Service.

stories.title: Watch: Horses evacuated as fire approaches riding centre near Paris
stories.summary: The Fontainebleau forest blaze, described by officials as of 'exceptional scale', continues to rage for the second day.

stories.title: Trump reimposes US blockade and demands 20% Hormuz shipping fee
stories.summary: Trump declared the US would collect a 20% fee on all Hormuz cargo as "Guardian of the Strait," flipping the script on Iran's own toll demands, while US forces struck dozens of targets in Iran and the IRGC hit Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait and Oman in the biggest exchange since the April ceasefire.

stories.title: Bison rams grandfather in Yellowstone Park, knocking him metres into the air
stories.summary: The man and his grandson appeared a safe distance away from the bison before it quickly chased them down.

stories.title: Briton tells of surviving Spain wildfire in car as wife and friends died trying to run to safety
stories.summary: Malcolm Timbrell's wife and their friends are thought to have died when a devastating wildfire tore through their village in Spain.

stories.title: US judge voids Donald Trump's $1.8bn settlement with IRS that gave him immunity from tax audits
stories.summary: The judge said the suit was brought for 'improper purposes' and referred a Trump attorney for possible disciplinary action.

stories.title: Identity of Sydney daycare worker facing 329 child abuse offences revealed
stories.summary: Hamish Tait is accused of abusing 136 children at five childcare facilities over a 16-year period.

stories.title: Australian police reveal unseen photos 25 years after British backpacker murder
stories.summary: Police hope the images could jog memories that will help them find the body of Peter Falconio.

stories.title: US burrito giant Chipotle opening first outlet in Mexico
stories.summary: Other US fast-food brands have struggled to succeed in the countries their dishes originated in.

stories.title: EU says threats against ICC 'unacceptable' as US launches campaign to 'dismantle' court
stories.summary: The Trump administration said it would pressure other countries to withdraw from the court, marking a sharp escalation in the US effort to isolate the Hague-based institution.

stories.title: How US commerce secretary's Epstein links were uncovered by British whistleblower
stories.summary: Simon Andriesz made the discovery about Howard Lutnick in publicly released Epstein files.

stories.title: Flowers and forensic teams at Bangkok bar where deadly fire killed at least 27
stories.summary: At least 27 people died and around 70 were injured in a bar fire in Bangkok, as police probe blocked exits and faulty wiring in the 50-year-old venue.

stories.title: King’s Swan Upping on the Thames shows healthy cygnets but rising litter
stories.summary: Traditional skiffs glided along the River Thames during Swan Upping as crews counted and weighed cygnets, reporting encouraging numbers while warning about the growing threat of river litter.

stories.title: ICE agent shoots dead Colombian immigrant in Maine
stories.summary: The 26-year-old, who had permission to work in the US, was killed in Biddeford when an officer opened fire, saying his car was a threat. It is the ninth death tied to Trump’s migration crackdown.

stories.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
stories.summary: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

stories.title: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
stories.summary: Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality rates There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial. It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists said, with pati

stories.title: Experts warn of ways screwworm could spread in the US and new difficulties in keeping it at bay
stories.summary: Scientists worry that current eradication efforts won’t be able to contain parasitic infestation pushing into US When conservationists set up cameras in remote regions of Central American forests, they wanted to monitor illegal cattle movement, which can lead to deforestation . But in recent months, they discovered another alarming development: wildlife rapidly infected with the new world screwworm. It’s a warning sign of how the fly could spread in the US – and it signals new difficulties in pu

stories.title: France and UK to increase staffing at border controls in effort to avert travel chaos
stories.summary: Disruption at Channel crossings expected to rise amid new fingerprint and facial recognition checks France and the UK have agreed to increase staffing at border controls in response to warnings of travel chaos caused by new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks. Disruption at Channel crossings is expected to rise sharply next weekend at the start of the summer holiday season, with MPs saying there would be “utter chaos and miles of tailbacks” unless the EU’s entry-exit system (EES) is fix

stories.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
stories.summary: The AfD is using the iconic GDR-era Simson moped in its campaign. Now, a Jewish heir to the founding family is fighting to protect the company's legacy.

stories.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
stories.summary: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

stories.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley: Cities on brink of financial collapse
stories.summary: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling. Oberhausen is particularly deep in the red.

stories.title: California's largest lake is turning to dust
stories.summary: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for nearby communities. Officials are racing to contain it, but for some families, it's already too late.

stories.title: Are German companies leaving the country?
stories.summary: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

stories.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy swaps out prime minister in reshuffle
stories.summary: Ukraine's president announced a government shake up, with PM Yuliia Svyrydenko stepping down after a year in office.

stories.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
stories.summary: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

stories.title: EU approves new passenger rights for air travel
stories.summary: EU ministers have approved a set of reforms giving more rights to air travelers. Among other things, compensation claims are to be made easier.

stories.title: Trump signals support for Patriot production in Ukraine
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he may let Ukraine produce Patriot air defense missiles. Analysts welcome the move as a sign of support for the country — but warn it could take years before production begins.

stories.title: Argentina fans erupt as Messi's men reach World Cup semifinal
stories.summary: Argentina fans celebrate in Buenos Aires after a dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over ten-man Switzerland sends the defending champions into the World Cup semifinals.

stories.title: Jude Bellingham double sends jubilant England into World Cup semis
stories.summary: England beat Norway 2-1 in extra time on 12 July 2026 to reach the World Cup semifinals. Fans celebrated in London after a Jude Bellingham double sent the Three Lions through to face Argentina.

stories.title: 'This landscape is completely charred': Inside the village at epicentre of Spain's wildfires
stories.summary: The BBC's Nick Beake sees homes razed to the ground and "a sea of black" in Bédar.

stories.title: France: hundreds dive into the Seine for Paris’ first river race in 80 years
stories.summary: Swimmers plunged into the Seine near the Eiffel Tower for the Open Swim Harmonie Mutuelle, the first major organised race on the river in nearly 80 years.

stories.title: Israel to hold elections on 27 October in what is seen as referendum on Netanyahu leadership
stories.summary: Israel will hold national elections on 27 October, the last date allowed by law to hold national elections. The Knesset is set to end its current term on 17 July.
